bank.attribution: The byte-sized REPL cursor/status correction and removal of two unused buffer bytes pay for the generated ordinal seam and reclaim a net 81 bytes. REPL_BUF_MAX is compile-time gated to 2..255.

symbols.attribution: Only %banner-run and %repl-banner remain resident internal symbols. %banner-runs and %banner-subtitle are privately inlined; no banner name is public or exported.

directory.attribution: The debit is eight aligned slots, not eight new functions: two internal entries move boot use from 208 to 210, and the mandatory align-to-eight step rounds 210 to 216. %banner-run is 248 bytes, already at the 255-byte object ceiling once call-site overhead is considered; inlining it into the 103-byte %repl-banner would violate the object-size gate.

overlay_vma.planning_effect: The measured 68-byte 1.1-L eventqueue path is now fundable with 14 bytes of projected overlay headroom remaining, subject to its own real link.

boot_overlay.accepted_reason: The overlay source and size are unchanged; absolute relocations move because the attributed REPL correction moves the VMA from c354 to c304. The wave repin covers the resulting product SHA.

accepted_contract_corrections: A minimal native seam launches the Lisp banner through a generated directory ordinal after scr_init and before the first prompt.
The explicit coordinate table is normative where the prose art disagrees.
The permanent visual oracle pins 235 visible writes rather than the approximate 180.
The subtitle uses WORKBENCH - DIALECT V2 until a PETSCII middle-dot mapping is measured and pinned.
Boot-overlay size remains neutral; VMA-driven byte nonidentity is accepted and must be repinned with the wave.

promotion_conditions.detector_calibration: must be confirmed against the real 235-write hardware rendering before wave seal

claim_limit: This authorization permits product-source promotion at the measured capacity. It does not accept hardware appearance, seal wave 1, or waive the regular R4/R5/R6/G6 repin.
